title: Rotary Encoder User-Interface Navigation
design_question: How to provide a compact, single-knob user interface for adjusting setpoints and navigating menus on an OLED-equipped embedded device?
selected_approach: KY-040 rotary encoder module (EC11 mechanical, 20 detents/rev, integral push-button). CLK/DT connected to interrupt-capable MCU GPIO; SW (push) to a third GPIO. External 10 nF capacitors to GND on CLK and DT for hardware debounce. State-machine ISR: tracks prior_CLK state to determine direction per detent.
rationale: Single-knob UI requires no display-keyboard matrix, fits in small enclosures, and is intuitive for numeric setpoint adjustment. EC11 with 20 detents gives satisfying tactile feedback. Push-to-confirm interaction eliminates need for separate OK button.

alternatives.reason_rejected: 16 keys occupy too much panel space for embedded instrument enclosures; requires 8 GPIO or I²C expander.
Sensitive to moisture and gloves; more complex firmware; harder to implement detent steps.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Rotary Encoder — Incremental A/B Quadrature Pulse Counting' confirms EC11 suitability, debounce requirement, and state-machine direction detection.
supporting_fact_titles: Rotary Encoder — Incremental A/B Quadrature Pulse Counting
